ROUTE: **Danny** (Lead/Architect) — PRD creation is an architecture & design task.

Let me have Danny draft the PRD for the todo app.Danny is drafting the PRD now. I'll share it once he's done.

PRD is ready. Let me show you the highlights:Danny's PRD is at **`PRD.md`**. Key highlights:

- **Single HTML file**, inline CSS + JS, no dependencies
- **Core features:** add, toggle done, delete, drag-to-reorder, "Clear done" button, localStorage persistence, empty state
- **Drag-drop:** HTML5 Drag and Drop API (no libraries); touch/mobile is a stretch goal
- **UI:** Centered single-column (~600px max), system fonts, WCAG AA contrast, keyboard-accessible
- **10 acceptance criteria** the team can test against
- **Explicitly out of scope:** auth, categories, due dates, undo/redo, dark mode toggle, sub-tasks, etc.

Ready for your review — let me know if you want any changes before the team starts building.

Now let me create the plan and kick off the build.Plan is set. Starting with **Rusty** on the base app.Here's the plan:

| # | Agent | Task | Status |
|---|-------|------|--------|
| 1 | **Rusty** | Base app — HTML/CSS/JS core (add, toggle, delete, clear done, localStorage, empty state) | 🔨 Building now |
| 2 | **Livingston** | Drag-and-drop reordering (HTML5 DnD API) | ⏳ Waiting on Rusty |
| 3 | **Danny** | Code review against PRD | ⏳ Waiting on Livingston |
| 4 | **Linus** | Test all 10 acceptance criteria | ⏳ Waiting on Danny |

Rusty is building the base app now. I'll hand off to Livingston as soon as he's done.

Livingston is wiring up drag-and-drop now. Will hand to Danny for review once done.

Livingston's done. Let me launch **Danny's review** and **Linus's testing** in parallel to move fast.Code looks complete. Launching **Danny** (review) and **Linus** (testing) in parallel.Both running in parallel now:
- 🔍 **Danny** — reviewing code against PRD
- 🧪 **Linus** — testing all 10 acceptance criteria + edge cases

Will report results as they come in.

Yes! All tasks complete:

- ✅ **PRD** written (`PRD.md`)
- ✅ **App** built (`index.html`) — single file, no dependencies
- ✅ **Danny** approved the code — no blockers
- ✅ **Linus** passed all 10 acceptance criteria

Open `index.html` in your browser and you're good to go. Want me to commit everything to git?

Good feature. Before building, a quick clarification — right now a todo is either active or done (checkbox toggle). Adding **cancelled** and **blocked** changes the interaction model. Let me get Danny to make a design call.Danny is designing the state model — needs to decide on UI interaction (dropdown vs checkbox vs cycle), visual treatment per state, and data migration. I'll share his ADR and updated PRD for your approval before Rusty builds it.

Here's **Danny's design** for the new states:

| State | Visual | Actionable? |
|-------|--------|-------------|
| **Active** | Default styling | Yes |
| **Done** | Strikethrough + grey | No (terminal) |
| **Cancelled** | Strikethrough + red | No (terminal) |
| **Blocked** | Orange left border + warm tint | Yes (waiting) |

**Key decisions:**
- **Checkbox → `<select>` dropdown** — one control, accessible for free, no hidden interactions
- **"Clear done" → "Clear finished"** — removes both done + cancelled (both terminal)
- **Data migration** — `done: bool` → `status: string` with automatic one-pass migration on load
- **No version field** — presence of `done` vs `status` is the version signal

Want me to proceed with implementation, or any changes to the design first?📋 **Ralph's Board — Idle**

| Agent | Last Task | Status |
|-------|-----------|--------|
| **Rusty** | Build base app | ✅ Done |
| **Livingston** | Drag-drop reordering | ✅ Done |

| **Linus** | Acceptance testing (10/10) | ✅ Done |

**No one is actively working.** Danny's multi-state design is ready and awaiting your approval to begin implementation. Say the word and Rusty + Livingston pick it up.
Got full context. Sending Rusty to implement with the complete ADR spec.Rusty is implementing the multi-state feature now. Once done, I'll launch Danny's review and Linus's testing in parallel.

**Blocked on you** — Danny's dependency design is ready for your review. Say "implement it" and Rusty picks it up.

Got it — no more gates, shipping continuously. Sending Rusty to implement dependencies now.🔨 **Rusty** is implementing dependencies now. Pipeline:
1. ~~Rusty: implement~~ 🔄 In progress
2. Danny + Linus: review + test (parallel, auto-launches when Rusty finishes)

Here's **Danny's design for task dependencies** in plain terms:

### What's changing

When you set a task to **Blocked**, you can now say *what* it's blocked by — picking from your other active tasks.

### Data model
Each todo gets an optional `blockedBy` array of task IDs:
```json
{ "id": "abc", "text": "Deploy app", "status": "blocked", "blockedBy": ["def", "ghi"] }
```
Only present when status is "blocked". Cleaned up otherwise.

### UI
- **Blocked items** get an inline checkbox panel listing other active/blocked tasks
- Check a task = "this blocks me". Uncheck = remove the dependency.
- A **subtitle** shows "Blocked by: Buy milk, Walk dog" on blocked items

### Smart auto-cleanup
| Event | What happens |
|-------|-------------|
| Blocker task completed/cancelled | Automatically removed from `blockedBy` |
| Blocker task deleted | Automatically removed from `blockedBy` |
| All blockers gone | Task auto-reverts to **active** |
| Status changed away from blocked | `blockedBy` cleared |
| "Clear finished" | Cleans up all references |

### What we're NOT doing
- No circular dependency detection (overkill for a todo app)
- No modals or popups — inline only
- No graph visualization

Rusty is implementing this right now. 🔄

❌ Something went wrong processing your message.

   • Run 'squad doctor' for diagnostics

This is an architecture decision question — routing to **Danny** (Lead), who owns architecture and technical decisions.Danny is reviewing the codebase and PRD to give you an informed recommendation. I'll share his take once he's done.

Here's **Danny's** recommendation:

---

**VERDICT: No — not now.**

The PRD explicitly bans frameworks (§2 Non-Goals, §6 Constraints: "no build step", "one .html file", "no external dependencies"). Even without that, a framework is over-engineering here — the entire app is ~330 lines of vanilla JS in a single file.

**What a framework would cost vs. buy:**
- 🔴 Violates 3 PRD constraints (build step, single-file, no deps)
- 🔴 40–140KB bundle size vs 0KB today
- 🟡 Only `render()` would benefit, and it's manageable at ~110 lines

**Revisit if 2+ of these become true:**
1. Multiple views/routes needed
2. Complex state graphs that break `render()`
3. Backend/API integration added
4. Team grows beyond 2 devs
5. PRD constraints are lifted

If we do revisit, Danny recommends evaluating **Preact** (3KB) or **Web Components** before full React.

**TL;DR:** The app is in vanilla JS's sweet spot. Ship what works. 🚀

No MCP servers are currently configured in this session. The available toolset consists of the built-in CLI tools (bash, view, edit, grep, glob, etc.) and the squad agents defined in `.squad/`.
